title: How Deploying a Digital Store on GitHub Pages Works and How to Set It Up Today — No PayPal Required
author: Unchained Commerce
excerpt: From zero to live crypto store in one afternoon. GitHub Pages hosting, Core Shop Engine, Web3 Wallet Connect, NOWPayments integration.

content.text: You're in Ethiopia, an incredible country with a rich culture, but a frustrating reality – being a digital creator here means circumventing PayPal, Stripe, and other gatekeepers, who claim 'due to restrictions, we cannot serve you.' Today, we break free from these digital shackles and deploy a store on GitHub Pages. Imagine selling your ebooks, courses, or art without relying on restrictive payment processors. With the right tools, you can finally reach your audience without the hindrances. Let's get started.
Why This Method Works When Everything Else Fails

Deploying a digital store on GitHub Pages is a game-changer for creators in restricted countries. Since you don't need a traditional payment processor, you bypass the KYC hurdles, and instant automated delivery means your customers get their products right away. The result? More sales, less hassle, and full control over your digital business. This method also allows for a more direct connection with your audience, as you're not intermediated by a third-party payment platform. You can choose to integrate Web3 Wallet Connect from Unchained Commerce to accept a wide range of cryptocurrencies.
What You Need Before You Start

To set up your GitHub Pages store, you'll need a GitHub account, a repository, and some basic coding knowledge. You can start by creating a new repository and choosing a theme for your store. Then, you'll need to integrate the Full Stack Bundle from Unchained Commerce into your repository. This bundle includes everything you need to get started, from the Core Shop Engine to the Admin Dashboard. Don't worry if you're not a developer – the installation process is straightforward, and there are plenty of resources available to help you.
Setting Up Your Payment and Delivery Pipeline

Once you've integrated the Full Stack Bundle, you'll need to set up your payment and delivery pipeline. This involves configuring the Core Shop Engine to work with your chosen cryptocurrency and setting up the Admin Dashboard to manage your orders. You'll also need to configure the automated delivery system to send your products to customers instantly. The good news is that the Full Stack Bundle makes this process seamless and easy to manage. You can also connect your Web3 Wallet Connect instance to accept multiple cryptocurrencies and integrate live price feeds into your store.
The key to success is to stay agile and adapt to your customers' needs.
Going Live and Making Your First Sale

After setting up your payment and delivery pipeline, it's time to go live and make your first sale. Test your store thoroughly to ensure everything is working as expected. Once you're confident, you can start promoting your store to your audience. Remember to provide clear instructions on how to purchase and download your products. With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have a seamless checkout process and instant automated delivery, making it easy for customers to buy from you. Don't be afraid to experiment and try new things – the key to success is to stay agile and adapt to your customers' needs.
Scaling Once the System Is Running

As your store grows, you'll need to scale your system to keep up with demand. This involves monitoring your traffic and sales, and optimizing your store's performance accordingly. You can use tools like Google Analytics to track your store's performance and identify areas for improvement. The Full Stack Bundle also includes a built-in Admin Dashboard that provides real-time analytics and transaction logs, making it easy to monitor your store's performance and make data-driven decisions.

How do I know if this method is right for me? If you're a digital creator in a restricted country struggling to get paid, this method is likely a good fit. The key is to understand that GitHub Pages and the Full Stack Bundle provide a flexible and scalable solution that can adapt to your business needs.
With the Full Stack Bundle, you'll have a seamless checkout process and instant automated delivery.

If you're interested in learning more about GitHub Pages, I recommend checking out their official documentation. Additionally, if you're looking to expand your digital products and services, consider exploring other tools and services from Unchained Commerce, such as Web3 Wallet Connect and the Admin Dashboard.
Your Action Checklist

To set up your GitHub Pages store with the Full Stack Bundle, follow these steps: Create a new GitHub repository and choose a theme for your store. Integrate the Full Stack Bundle into your repository. Configure the Core Shop Engine to work with your chosen cryptocurrency. Set up the Admin Dashboard to manage your orders. Configure the automated delivery system to send your products to customers instantly. Test your store thoroughly before going live. Promote your store to your audience and provide clear instructions on how to purchase and download your products.
The result is a scalable and flexible digital store that can adapt to your business needs.
